stem: A 10-year-old child is experiencing severe post-operative hemorrhage, but the parents refuse a life-saving blood transfusion on religious grounds. Based on the EHR, identify the overriding legal principle, two priority nursing actions, and two parameters to monitor.

tabs.title: SBAR
tabs.content:
S: 07:15. This is the nurse for the 10-year-old post-appendectomy patient. The patient is experiencing severe hemorrhage, and the parents, who are Jehovah's Witnesses, are refusing a blood transfusion.
B: The patient's hemoglobin just came back critical at 4.2 g/dL. ABG shows metabolic acidosis with a pH of 7.21.
A: The patient is lethargic, with a BP of 72/40, heart rate of 158, and respiratory rate of 32. The parents are adamant about their refusal.
R: I need you to confirm the plan to seek an emergency court order for the transfusion. I am continuing crystalloid resuscitation now.

tabs.title: Legal & Ethics Log
tabs.content:
Legal DoctrineStatusNotes
Parental AutonomyApplies (Initial)Parents have the right to refuse care normally.
Parens PatriaeInvoked (Emergency)State's right to protect minors from harm/death.
Judicial ConsentPending / EmergentCourt order being sought by hospital legal team.
Hgb Threshold4.2 g/dL (Critical)Life-threat established.

tabs.title: Vital Signs
tabs.content:
Time: 07:15
BP: 72/40 mmHg
HR: 158/min
RR: 32/min
SpO2: 91% on room air
Temp: 36.5°C (97.7°F)
Note: Patient is pale, cool to the touch, and lethargic.

tabs.title: Laboratory Results
tabs.content:
Complete Blood Count (CBC)
Hemoglobin: 4.2 g/dL (Critical Low)
Hematocrit: 12.6% (Critical Low)

Arterial Blood Gas (ABG)
pH: 7.21 (Acidosis)
PaCO2: 30 mmHg
HCO3: 14 mEq/L

conditionOptions.hoverRationale: This is incorrect. While parental autonomy is respected, it is not absolute when a decision places a minor's life in imminent danger. The state's interest in preserving the child's life (parens patriae) overrides parental religious objections in such emergencies.

This is correct. The legal doctrine of parens patriae allows the state to intervene and act as the parent to protect a child from harm when parents are unwilling or unable to do so, especially in life-threatening situations.

conditionOptions.text: Medical Neglect by the Hospital Team
conditionOptions.hoverRationale: This is incorrect. The hospital team is actively seeking to provide life-saving care and is following legal and ethical protocols to do so. Medical neglect would involve failing to act or provide appropriate care.

actionOptions.text: Notify legal/ethics for an emergency court order.
actionOptions.hoverRationale: This is a correct action. Involving the hospital's legal and ethics teams is the proper procedure to obtain judicial authorization to administer life-saving treatment against parental consent, protecting both the child and the clinical staff.

This is a correct action. While awaiting the court order, the nurse must provide supportive care. Administering non-blood volume expanders like crystalloids helps stabilize the patient's hemodynamic status by increasing intravascular volume.

actionOptions.text: Administer blood without a court order.
actionOptions.hoverRationale: This is an incorrect action. Administering treatment against parental refusal without legal authority constitutes battery. This could result in legal action against the nurse and the hospital, despite the good intention.

actionOptions.text: Initiate comfort care per parents' wishes.
actionOptions.hoverRationale: This is an incorrect action. The child has a treatable, life-threatening condition. The nurse's primary duty is to advocate for the child's life, which requires pursuing treatment, not withdrawing care and allowing the child to die.

parameterOptions.hoverRationale: This is a correct parameter. Monitoring blood pressure, heart rate, and hemoglobin is critical to assess the severity of hemorrhage and the patient's response to volume resuscitation, guiding further interventions.

parameterOptions.text: Status of the emergency court order
parameterOptions.hoverRationale: This is a correct parameter. The ability to administer the definitive treatment (blood transfusion) is contingent on legal approval. The nurse must track the progress of the court order to act as soon as it is granted.

parameterOptions.text: Daily measurement of fasting serum glucose
parameterOptions.hoverRationale: This is an incorrect parameter. While glucose is important, it is not a priority parameter for monitoring a patient with acute hemorrhagic shock. The focus must be on perfusion and oxygen-carrying capacity.

parameterOptions.text: Presence of a positive Babinski sign
parameterOptions.hoverRationale: This is an incorrect parameter. A Babinski sign assesses upper motor neuron function and is not relevant to monitoring hemorrhagic shock. The priority is assessing cardiovascular and hematologic status.
